Whenever a creature drinks an experimental elixir you created, the creature gains temporary hit points equal to 2d6 + your Intelligence modifier. You can also cast Lesser Restoration without expending a spell slot and without preparing the spell, provided you use alchemist's supplies as the spellcasting focus. You can do so a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Chemical Mastery
features.description: You have been exposed to so many chemicals that they have affected your body beneficially: you gain resistance to acid damage and poison damage, and you are immune to the poisoned condition. You can also cast Greater Restoration and Heal each once without expending a spell slot, without preparing the spells, and without providing the material component, provided you use alchemist's supplies as the spellcasting focus. Once you cast either spell with this feature, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a long rest.

artificer.name: Armorer
artificer.description: An Armorer uses arcane power to shape metal into a protective force, infusing their armor with magical enhancements. An Armorer in battle is a force to be reckoned with, combining the best of martial and magical ability.

features.name: Tool Proficiency, Armorer Spells & Arcane Armor
features.description: You gain proficiency with heavy armor. You also gain proficiency with smith's tools. Your spells list expands with: Magic Missile, Thunderwave (3rd); Mirror Image, Shatter (5th); Hypnotic Pattern, Lightning Bolt (9th); Fire Shield, Greater Invisibility (13th); Passwall, Wall of Force (17th). Arcane Armor: as an action, you can turn a suit of armor you are wearing into Arcane Armor, provided you have smith's tools in hand. The armor can't be removed against your will; you can don or doff it as an action; and if it lacks a helmet, gloves, or boots, the armor magically creates those attachments. The armor continues to be Arcane Armor until you don another suit of armor or die. Choose a model: Guardian (thunder gauntlets, defensive field bonus action) or Infiltrator (+5 ft speed, 1d6 lightning damage unarmed, lightning launcher ranged 90 ft 1d6 lightning).

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice, rather than once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

features.name: Armor Modifications
features.description: You learn how to use your Infuse Item feature to infuse your Arcane Armor. The armor now counts as separate items for the purpose of your Infuse Items feature: chest piece, boots, helmet, and the armor's special weapon. Each of those items can bear one of your infusions, and the infusions transfer over if you change your armor's model with the Armor Model feature. In addition, the maximum number of items you can infuse at once increases by 2, but those extra items must be part of your Arcane Armor.

features.name: Perfected Armor
features.description: Your Arcane Armor gains additional benefits based on its model. Guardian: when a Huge or smaller creature you can see ends its turn within 30 ft of you, you can use your reaction to magically force it to make a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, it is pulled up to 30 ft toward you, then you can make one melee attack against it as part of this reaction. Infiltrator: any creature that takes lightning damage from your lightning launcher glimmers with magical light until the start of your next turn. The glimmering creature sheds dim light in a 5-ft radius, and attack rolls against it have advantage.

artificer.name: Artillerist
artificer.description: An Artillerist specializes in using magic to hurl energy, projectiles, and explosions on a battlefield. This destructive power was valued by all armies in the wars of the past. Now, some members of this specialization use their talents as adventurers or as guardians of those who can't protect themselves.

features.name: Tool Proficiency, Artillerist Spells & Eldritch Cannon
features.description: You gain proficiency with woodcarver's tools. Your spells list expands with: Shield, Thunderwave (3rd); Scorching Ray, Shatter (5th); Fireball, Wind Wall (9th); Ice Storm, Wall of Fire (13th); Cone of Cold, Wall of Force (17th). Eldritch Cannon: using woodcarver's tools or smith's tools, you create a Tiny or Small magical cannon. The cannon appears in an unoccupied space on a horizontal surface within 5 ft of you. As a bonus action, you can activate it (requires line of sight within 60 ft). It is a magical object with AC 18 and hit points equal to five times your artificer level, immune to poison and psychic damage. Choose: Flamethrower (15-ft cone, Dex save DC = spell save DC, 2d8 fire on fail/half on success), Force Ballista (60-ft ranged spell attack, 2d8 force, push 5 ft), or Protector (10-ft radius temporary hit points equal to 1d8 + Intelligence modifier, including yourself). Each cannon lasts 1 hour or until reduced to 0 hp. You can create one cannon per long rest (plus one per spell slot used). You can't have more than one active at a time.

features.name: Arcane Firearm
features.description: You know how to turn a wand, staff, or rod into an Arcane Firearm. When you finish a long rest, you can use woodcarver's tools to carve special sigils into a wand, staff, or rod and thereby turn it into your Arcane Firearm. The firearm remains such until you use this feature again. When you make a damage roll with a spell using the Arcane Firearm as your focus, you can roll a d8 and add it to the total damage.

features.name: Explosive Cannon
features.description: Every Eldritch Cannon you create is more destructive. Damage rolls of the cannon increase by 1d8 (Flamethrower: 3d8; Force Ballista: 3d8). As an action, you can command the cannon to detonate if you are within 60 ft of it. Doing so destroys the cannon and forces each creature within 20 ft of it to make a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC, taking 3d8 force damage on a failed save or half as much on a successful one.

features.name: Fortified Position
features.description: You and your allies have half cover while within 10 ft of a Eldritch Cannon you create with Eldritch Cannon, taking into account the direction from which the attack came. You can also now have two cannons at the same time. You can create two with the same action (but still pay for each), and you can activate both of them with the same bonus action. You determine whether the cannons are identical to each other or different.

artificer.name: Battle Smith
artificer.description: Armies require skilled smiths to keep weapons and armor in fighting trim, and those who combine their metalworking skills with a keen fighting ability are true battle smiths. Trusted by soldiers, a battle smith is a combination of protector and medic, a skilled fighter in their own right.

features.name: Tool Proficiency, Battle Smith Spells, Battle Ready & Steel Defender
features.description: You gain proficiency with smith's tools. Your spells list expands with: Heroism, Shield (3rd); Branding Smite, Warding Bond (5th); Aura of Vitality, Conjure Barrage (9th); Aura of Purity, Fire Shield (13th); Banishing Smite, Mass Cure Wounds (17th). Battle Ready: you gain proficiency with martial weapons, and you can use your Intelligence modifier instead of Strength or Dexterity when making attack and damage rolls with magic weapons. Steel Defender: you create a steel defender, a mechanical companion (stat block varies by level). As a bonus action, you can command it to take the Dash, Disengage, Help, or Attack action. It acts on your initiative. If destroyed, you can recreate it during a long rest with 1 hour of work and smith's tools.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice, rather than once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

features.name: Arcane Jolt
features.description: You learn new ways to channel arcane energy to harm or heal. When either you or the steel defender hits a target with a magic weapon attack, you can channel magical energy through the strike to create one of the following effects: the target takes 2d6 extra force damage, or a creature or object you can see within 30 ft of the target restores 2d6 hit points. You can use this energy a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum once), but you can do so no more than once on a turn. You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Improved Defender
features.description: Your Arcane Jolt and Steel Defender become more powerful. The extra damage and the healing of your Arcane Jolt both increase to 4d6. Your steel defender gains a +2 bonus to Armor Class. Whenever your steel defender uses its Deflect Attack, the attacker takes force damage equal to 1d4 + your Intelligence modifier.
